ops.title: The local HuggingFace token (yasyf-home) is role:read — hf whoami looks fine but repo creation/push 403s; user must re-auth with a write token
ops.body:
The `hf` CLI on this machine (mise pipx install) is authed as user `yasyf` (org
`anetaco`) with access token **"yasyf-home", role `read`** (created 2026-03-30, stored
at `~/.cache/huggingface/token`). `hf auth whoami` succeeds, so it LOOKS authed, but
`create_repo`/`push_to_hub` fail `403 Forbidden: You don't have the rights to create a
dataset under the namespace "yasyf"`. Diagnose with
`GET https://huggingface.co/api/whoami-v2` → `auth.accessToken.role`.

**Why:** read tokens satisfy whoami and downloads; any write (dataset push, repo
create) needs a `write` or fine-grained token with repo-write scope.

**How to apply:** before any `push_to_hub`/upload, check the role first; if `read`,
ask the user to run `! hf auth login` with a write token (or export `HF_TOKEN`).
Used by `cc-pushback export --push` → private `yasyf/cc-pushback-traces`
([[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]]).
